The Context Platform Stack
by Paula Silva
Formats The stack Articles About
A framework for AI-native systems

Four layers of engineering discipline to prevent the agent cemetery.

Why pilots work in the lab and die in production, and the smallest set of patterns that prevents it. A playbook, a deck, diagrams, and field notes - all built on the same framework.

Read the playbook See all formats
Read it, present it, build with it

Four formats, one framework.

The same four-layer model, adapted to how you need to engage with it. Start with the playbook for depth. Use the deck for stakeholders. Pull diagrams into your docs. Read the field notes for the current thinking.

Playbook · v1.2.0

The Context Platform Stack

Six chapters, fully annotated. The complete framework: why pilots die in production, the three forms of AI-native debt, and the four layers of discipline that prevent the cemetery. Intended for Platform Engineering Leads and AI-Native Tech Leads carrying 5 to 15 years of systems experience.

HTML · ~16,000 words · ~90 min read
Deck · 19 slides

AI Maturity Assessment

60 to 90 minute presentation for enterprise leadership. Seven dimensions, four maturity levels, one prioritized roadmap. PT, EN and ES built in.

HTML · keyboard-navigable
Diagrams · 13 components

Diagram Library

LayerPyramid, PillarGrid, TierStack, CycleLoop and nine more. Accessible SVG components with typed props. Copy into your own docs, keep the DS intact.

Astro components · SVG preview
Field notes · ongoing

Articles & Field Notes

Shorter pieces responding to what is actually breaking in production right now. KubeCon observations, practitioner interviews, and corrections to what the playbook got wrong.

Updated monthly
The stack at a glance

Each layer serves the one above. Each constrains the one below.

The four layers are co-dependent, not sequential. A platform without intent capture produces scaled chaos. Intent without context engineering produces specifications nobody can build to. Context without platform produces islands of capability. Infrastructure alone goes nowhere.

04
Intent engineering Constitutions, specifications, governance
What should the agent optimize for?
Chapter 4
03
Context engineering ACE, skills, memory tiers, MCP
What can the agent know?
Chapter 3
02
Platform engineering IDP, golden paths, guardrails, RBAC
What can agents do, and who governs them?
Chapter 2
01
Cloud & infrastructure GPUs, Kubernetes, CNCF services
Where do agents run, and at what cost?
Chapter 1
Field notes

Current thinking.

Shorter pieces that respond to what is actually happening in production. Updated as new data comes in from KubeCon, from practitioners, and from the accounts I work with.

Infrastructure · 8 min

Why Kubernetes already won the AI workload fight

The DORA data shows ~90 percent of enterprises now operate internal platforms. Kubernetes isn't a bet anymore, it's the substrate. What changes when the workloads on top are agentic.

April 2026
 Platform · 6 min

Spotify's 1,500+ agent PRs, and what the pattern actually is

The headline number gets repeated. The infrastructure underneath it, the golden paths and RBAC that made it possible, does not. A breakdown of what scaled and what didn't.

March 2026
 Context · 10 min

AGENTS.md is context engineering, not documentation

Human-curated AGENTS.md achieves 28.64 percent runtime reduction. LLM-generated versions perform 3 percent worse than none. Why curation is the job, not the overhead.

March 2026
 Intent · 7 min

The Klarna lesson: context without intent is expensive to be wrong

Klarna had everything context engineering would tell you to build. The agent still optimized for the wrong thing. Because intent was never captured in artifacts, it had no opinion to enforce.

February 2026
About

Who wrote this, and why.

I'm Paula, Software Global Black Belt at Microsoft. My work sits between platform engineering, AI infrastructure, and the practical reality of helping teams ship agentic systems that don't die three weeks after the pilot demo.

Across dozens of enterprise deployments I kept seeing the same failure mode: teams would optimize the pilot, declare victory, and then discover that what got them to the proof-of-concept was exactly what would not get them to production. This playbook is the set of patterns I wish I had been handed five years ago.

Everything here is free to read, adapt, and reuse. If it is useful in your team, the best thanks is to tell me what broke.

Paula Silva
Software Global Black Belt
  • Based in Brazil · Latam coverage
  • Contact paulasilva@microsoft.com

The Context Platform Stack

A framework for building AI-native systems that survive production. Four interdependent layers of engineering discipline, adapted for enterprise context.

Companion formats: playbook, deck, diagram library, field notes.

Playbook Deck Diagrams Field notes
